zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Stowford於大約1308年出生在英國戴文斯托福德時，她的父親John Stowford29歲，母親Isabella DeTracy33歲。她和Brightley至少有1個女兒。她去世於1346年在英國英格蘭戴文布拉德費爾德享年39歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Stowford于大约1308年出生在英国戴文斯托福德时，她的父亲John Stowford29岁，母亲Isabella DeTracy33岁。她和Brightley至少有1个女儿。她去世于1346年在英国英格兰戴文布拉德費爾德享年39岁。

nameMeanings.name: Stowford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Stockport (Cheshire), which is recorded as Stokeport in 1188, Stockford in 1283–8, Stopford in 1347, Stoppord in 1337, and Stapporte in 1583.

variants.name: Stopper
meanings: English: perhaps a nickname for someone with a heavy stride or an awkward way of walking, from a conjectural Middle English word stauper, stouper or, with substitution of the pejorative suffix -ard, Middle English staupard, stoupard.

English: perhaps an occupational name for a maker of stoups, from Middle English staupe, stoupe, stope (Old Norse staup, reinforced by Middle Dutch stoop), ‘jug, often made of leather; measure for liquids; vessel or container for holy water’. Alternatively, the name may derive from an unrecorded Middle English stopper, perhaps ‘maker of pails, buckets, or jugs’, a derivative of Middle English stoppe (Old English stoppa).

variants.name: Stobbart
meanings: English: relationship name from the Middle English personal name Stubard, Stobard, late Old English * Stubheard, recorded only in E Anglia, so the surname in post-medieval NE England may be due to migration from there via the east coast trade route. The post-medieval surname may have been confused with Stappard and Stopford , and in E Anglia it may have been absorbed into Stibbard .

variants.name: Stappard
meanings: English: (i) nickname, perhaps for someone with a heavy stride or an awkward way of walking, from a conjectural Middle English word * stauper, * stouper or, with substitution of the pejorative suffix -ard, Middle English * staupard, * stoupard. Compare 19th-century Whitby (NR Yorks) dialect stauper ‘a clumsy fellow, a clown’, from the dialect verb staup, stowp, stoop ‘to walk clumsily and heavily’, which is recorded in English Dialect Dictionary from Northumb and Cumb southwards as far as Derbys and Lincs. The name was sometimes confused with Stobbart and perhaps with Stopford . (ii) occupational name, perhaps for a maker of stoups. * Stauper, * Stouper, and * Stoper would be unrecorded derivatives of Middle English * staupe, stoupe, stope (Old Scandinavian staup, reinforced by Middle Dutch stoop), ‘a jug, often made of leather; a measure for liquids; a vessel or container for holy water’. This explanation accounts for the variation between Stauper, Stoper, Stooper, and possibly Stopper, but not the forms with final -d, -t or -th, unless through false association with other names, such as (1) above.

variants.name: Stockford
meanings: English (Oxfordshire): in northern England and the North Midlands, usually a variant of Stockport, a habitational name from Stockport (Cheshire), from Old English stoc ‘place, secondary settlement’ + port ‘town, market’; elsewhere it may have a different origin, such as Stokeford in East Stoke (Dorset), which takes its name from East Stoke + Old English ford ‘ford’, meaning ‘the ford near (East) Stoke’.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Stowford was born about 1308, in Stowford, Devon, England, her father, John Stowford, was 29 and her mother, Isabella DeTracy, was 33. She had at least 1 daughter with Brightley. She died in 1346, in Bradfield, Devon, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 39.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Stowford est née environ 1308, à Stowford, Devon, Angleterre, son père, John Stowford, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Isabella DeTracy, avait 33 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Brightley. Elle est décédée en 1346, à Bradfield, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 39 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Stowford um 1308 in Stowford, Devon, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Stowford 29 und ihre Mutter Isabella DeTracy 33. Sie hatte mit Brightley mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1346 im Alter von 39 Jahren in Bradfield, Devon, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Stowford è nata il circa 1308 nel Stowford, Devon, England, sua padre, John Stowford, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Isabella DeTracy, aveva 33 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Brightley. Morì nel 1346, nel Bradfield, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 39 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Stowford(이)가 대략 1308년 영국 데폰 스타우퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Stowford(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabella DeTracy(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 Brightley(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1346년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 브래드필드에서 39세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Stowford родилась около 1308 г. в местности стоуфорд, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени John Stowford было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabella DeTracy, 33.
